steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

steps.target: to be known / to be recognized
choices: to be known / to be recognized

hints.present active indicative: Active would be 'cognoscit' — the -tur ending marks passive voice
hints.perfect passive: Perfect passive would be 'cognitus est' — 'cognoscitur' is present passive
hints.imperfect passive: Imperfect passive would be 'cognoscebatur' — 'cognoscitur' is present

hints.cogito, cogitare, cogitavi, cogitatum: These are principal parts of 'cogito' (to think)
hints.cogo, cogere, coegi, coactum: These are principal parts of 'cogo' (to compel)
hints.colligo, colligere, collegi, collectum: These are principal parts of 'colligo' (to gather)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'arborem' — 'arbor' is the nominative subject
hints.vocative: Vocative would also be 'arbor' but this is a statement, not an address

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'arboris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'arboris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'arboris' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative is 'fructus' — 'fructu' is the ablative singular (by its fruit)
hints.accusative: Accusative is 'fructum' — 'fructu' is ablative, governed by 'ex'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'fructus' has genitive in -us
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns in -us have genitive in -i — 'fructus' has genitive 'fructus'
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'fructus' has genitive 'fructus' (-us)

translation: Every tree is known by its own fruit
